tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Young Man Rings the Bell: Sickle Cell Disease Functionally Cured Through Gene Therapy
stories.summary: Daniel Cressy, 23, rings the bell after two years of gene therapy frees him from sickle cell disease, finally pursuing his dream of becoming a commercial pilot.

stories.headline: From Sacrifice to Glory: Goalkeeper's World Cup Heroics After Selling His Boots to Save His Son
stories.summary: Orlando Gill, Paraguay's penalty shootout hero against Germany, once sacrificed everything—including his football boots—to save his son's life.

stories.headline: Love in Action: Couple Spends 14 Hours Daily Caring for 51 Evacuated Dogs
stories.summary: A devoted couple spends 14 hours daily caring for 51 evacuated dogs, ensuring frightened pets left behind during wildfire evacuation have food, water, and comfort.

stories.headline: Bloodied but Unstoppable: US Star's Clutch Free Kick Writes World Cup Legend
stories.summary: Malik Tillman, fighting years to earn his spot, scores a stunning free kick with a torn boot and bloodied sock in a crucial World Cup moment.

stories.headline: Sixty-One Rhinos Find New Hope in Mozambique's Transformed Sanctuary
stories.summary: Sixty-one rhinos—39 white and 22 black—now thrive in Mozambique's Zinave National Park, restoring hope after the country lost virtually all large wildlife during civil war.

stories.headline: Two Young Heroes Stop Their Car to Rescue Family From Burning Home
stories.summary: Mohsin Qayyum, 22, and Mohammed Yusuf Iqbal, 20, stopped their car to rescue an elderly couple and their grandson from a burning home in Leeds, then humbly downplayed their heroism.

stories.headline: From Nonprofit Academy to World Cup Glory: Julián Quiñones Embodies the Power of Belonging
stories.summary: Julián Quiñones, born in Colombia and raised in a nonprofit academy, scores and assists Mexico toward World Cup history, embodying the power of belonging and resilience.

stories.headline: Harry Kane's Late Heroics Lift England From the Brink in World Cup Thriller
stories.summary: England captain Harry Kane scores a dramatic two-goal brace in the final minutes, rescuing the Three Lions from near-certain elimination.

stories.headline: Rare Coastal Caterpillars Return Home: Conservation Success for Endangered Butterflies
stories.summary: Conservationists release 1,300 carefully bred Oregon silverspot caterpillars to wild coastal habitats, giving an endangered species a fighting chance.

stories.headline: One Woman's Passion Sparked a Community Cleanup Movement
stories.summary: Bailey King's mission to clean up litter inspired dozens of neighbors to join her weekly efforts, transforming her parish one pickup at a time.

stories.headline: Ancient Alpine Home Welcomes Back Thousands of Boreal Toad Tadpoles
stories.summary: Colorado Parks and Wildlife release over 3,500 endangered boreal toad tadpoles into historic alpine wetlands near Leadville, restoring a species to its native home.
